perturbation.original_text: 1.10 "Control" or "Controlled" shall mean possession of more than fifty percent (50%) of the share capital of a corporation or other business entity, and/or the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of a corporation or other entity whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.10 "Control" or "Controlled" shall mean possession of more than twenty percent (20%) of the share capital of a corporation or other business entity, and/or the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of a corporation or other entity whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the definition of "Control" to 20% introduces an in-text contradiction. The definition initially sets a clear threshold of 50% ownership to constitute control, while later on it may be creating issues with change of control
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.6 "Change of Control" shall mean (i) any change, sale, merger, reorganization, or any other event or action that results in a third party, which is a material competitor to the other Party to this agreement, acquiring: (a) all or substantially all of the business or assets of a Party relating to this Agreement, (b) Control, directly or indirectly, of such Party (and/or any corporate entity that Controls, directly or indirectly, such Party), or (ii) any assignment or delegation of, sale or transfer of a Party's rights and obligations under this Agreement (or any part hereof) to a third party. Notwithstanding anything in the immediately preceding paragraph to the contrary, where the Party in question is Dexcel, any of the foregoing events or actions shall not be considered a Change of Control where any one or more of the relevant third party or parties referred to in clause (i) above is (A) a Family Member, or (B) any entity Controlled by Mr. **** and/or a Family Member.
perturbation.justification: The change in the definition of "Control" from 50% to 20% directly contradicts the "Change of Control" clause. The original definition provided a clear threshold for determining control. Lowering this threshold introduces uncertainty and potential disputes regarding when a change of control occurs, especially concerning the exceptions related to Family Members and entities Controlled by Mr. **** and/or a Family Member. This makes the contract more difficult to enforce and creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.7.2 Kitov shall provide Dexcel with written notification of any shortfalls in shipment quantity, and (a) any out-of-specification temperature excursions based on the downloaded data logger information following compliance with the provisions of the Quality Agreement, and/or (b) any failure of the Product to meet the Specifications which are apparent upon visual inspection and/or identification testing of the Product delivered to it by Dexcel (each of (a) and (b) being an "Apparent Defect"), such notification to be provided within thirty (30) Working Days of receipt of the Product at Kitov's warehouse, accompanied by samples of any such allegedly defective Product and any such Product shall not be removed from quarantine until their status is resolved. In the event that a defect is not apparent upon visual inspection during the shelf life of the Product ("Hidden Defect"), Kitov shall use commercially reasonably best efforts to provide Dexcel with written notification within thirty (30) Working Days of discovering the same, to be accompanied by samples of any such allegedly defective Product, if such samples are available In the event of any failure by Kitov to provide Dexcel with written notification of any such shortfall, Apparent Defect or Hidden Defect within the respective aforementioned periods, it shall be deemed as Kitov having accepted the relevant consignment.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.7.2 Kitov shall provide Dexcel with written notification of any shortfalls in shipment quantity, and (a) any out-of-specification temperature excursions based on the downloaded data logger information following compliance with the provisions of the Quality Agreement, and/or (b) any failure of the Product to meet the Specifications which are apparent upon visual inspection and/or identification testing of the Product delivered to it by Dexcel (each of (a) and (b) being an "Apparent Defect"), such notification to be provided within one-hundred and eighty (180) Working Days of receipt of the Product at Kitov's warehouse, accompanied by samples of any such allegedly defective Product and any such Product shall not be removed from quarantine until their status is resolved. In the event that a defect is not apparent upon visual inspection during the shelf life of the Product ("Hidden Defect"), Kitov shall use commercially reasonably best efforts to provide Dexcel with written notification within thirty (30) Working Days of discovering the same, to be accompanied by samples of any such allegedly defective Product, if such samples are available In the event of any failure by Kitov to provide Dexcel with written notification of any such shortfall, Apparent Defect or Hidden Defect within the respective aforementioned periods, it shall be deemed as Kitov having accepted the relevant consignment.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the notification timeframe for "Apparent Defect" from thirty (30) to one-hundred and eighty (180) Working Days creates an in-text contradiction. This extended timeframe for Apparent Defects clashes with the shorter thirty (30) day timeframe for Hidden Defects. This discrepancy leads to confusion regarding the acceptance of consignments and the conditions under which defects are discovered, directly contradicting the concluding statement of the paragraph and potentially hindering the product acceptance process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.7.2 Kitov shall provide Dexcel with written notification of any shortfalls in shipment quantity, and (a) any out-of-specification temperature excursions based on the downloaded data logger information following compliance with the provisions of the Quality Agreement, and/or (b) any failure of the Product to meet the Specifications which are apparent upon visual inspection and/or identification testing of the Product delivered to it by Dexcel (each of (a) and (b) being an "Apparent Defect"), such notification to be provided within one-hundred and eighty (180) Working Days of receipt of the Product at Kitov's warehouse, accompanied by samples of any such allegedly defective Product and any such Product shall not be removed from quarantine until their status is resolved. In the event that a defect is not apparent upon visual inspection during the shelf life of the Product ("Hidden Defect"), Kitov shall use commercially reasonably best efforts to provide Dexcel with written notification within thirty (30) Working Days of discovering the same, to be accompanied by samples of any such allegedly defective Product, if such samples are available In the event of any failure by Kitov to provide Dexcel with written notification of any such shortfall, Apparent Defect or Hidden Defect within the respective aforementioned periods, it shall be deemed as Kitov having accepted the relevant consignment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the notification timeframe for 'Apparent Defects' to one-hundred and eighty (180) Working Days, while retaining a thirty (30) Working Day timeframe for 'Hidden Defects'. This creates a direct conflict within section 3.7.2 itself and with the concluding statement in the same paragraph, 'In the event of any failure by Kitov to provide Dexcel with written notification of any such shortfall, Apparent Defect or Hidden Defect within the respective aforementioned periods, it shall be deemed as Kitov having accepted the relevant consignment.' The different timeframes for reporting 'Apparent' versus 'Hidden' defects, especially given that the acceptance of the consignment depends on these notification periods, creates an in-text contradiction under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 The Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and remain in full force and effect for an initial term of **** from the Supply Commencement Date of the Product ("Initial Term"). Following the Initial Term, the Agreement shall automatically be renewed for additional periods of **** (each, a "Renewal Term," and, together with the Initial Term, the "Term")), unless a Party provides written notification of non-renewal to the other Party at least **** of the Initial Term or a Renewal Term.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 The Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and remain in full force and effect for an initial term of **** from the Supply Commencement Date of the Product ("Initial Term"). Following the Initial Term, the Agreement shall automatically be renewed for additional periods of **** (each, a "Renewal Term," and, together with the Initial Term, the "Term")), unless a Party provides written notification of non-renewal to the other Party at least **** of the Initial Term, but it will automatically renew regardless of notification during a Renewal Term.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of the clause 'but it will automatically renew regardless of notification during a Renewal Term' creates an in-text contradiction. The initial condition states that the contract renews unless notification of non-renewal is provided, making it conditional, while the added part states that the contract will automatically renew regardless of notification specifically during Renewal Terms, creating a conflicting conditional statement on automatic renewel that can be confusing and can be cause for a dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 The Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and remain in full force and effect for an initial term of **** from the Supply Commencement Date of the Product ("Initial Term"). Following the Initial Term, the Agreement shall automatically be renewed for additional periods of **** (each, a "Renewal Term," and, together with the Initial Term, the "Term")), unless a Party provides written notification of non-renewal to the other Party at least **** of the Initial Term, but it will automatically renew regardless of notification during a Renewal Term.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 5.1 introduces a contradiction. The original text establishes a conditional renewal based on notification of non-renewal. The addition of 'but it will automatically renew regardless of notification during a Renewal Term' creates a direct conflict, making the renewal both conditional and unconditional, specifically during renewal terms. This contradicts the initial condition, as notification of non-renewal should prevent renewal, but the added clause nullifies this during Renewal Terms. The contradicted text is the changed text itself: 'The Agreement shall commence...Renewal Term' because the addition to that section directly creates the contradiction.
